title: Decadent Greek Pork Stir‑fry
description: A salty, velvety, low‑sodium Greek stir‑fry featuring tender pork back ribs and fresh vegetables, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.

ingredients.name: pork back ribs, trimmed and cut into 1‑inch cubes

instructions: Trim the pork back ribs and cut into 1‑inch cubes.
In a bowl whisk together soy sauce, Greek yogurt, lemon juice, oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper to create a quick sauce.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat and sear pork cubes until browned all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add onion and bell pepper to the skillet and sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Pour the prepared sauce over the pork and vegetables, stir to coat, and reduce heat to medium.
Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until pork is tender and sauce thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, remembering the low‑sodium focus.
Plate the stir‑fry over a bed of steamed white rice or couscous and drizzle with extra lemon juice if desired.
Garnish with fresh parsley or dill for brightness (optional).
Serve hot and enjoy the velvety, salty Greek flavor.
